stdout:
I can‚Äôt execute shell commands here, so I‚Äôm doing a full file-level audit of the relevant Rust and Lean sources plus gate history evidence.


### Success criteria + prior feedback + dependencies
- `issue.dependencies` is empty, so no prerequisite blockers.
- Phase 1 appears met (`scripts/verify-lean.sh` uses `--no-default-features`; `proofs/WORKAROUNDS.md` documents blockers/workarounds).
- Phase 2 (`mul_raw`) is largely present (`proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/Gf2mMulRaw.lean`).
- **Phase 3 is not met**: `proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/Gf2mInverse.lean:167` still contains `sorry`, so inverse correctness is unfinished.
- Most recent prior feedback remains unresolved: inverse proof incompleteness, API contract ambiguity, missing `m=65`/property coverage.

### Separation of concerns
- `gf2-core` has no dependency on `gf2-coding` (`crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml`), while `gf2-coding` depends on `gf2-core` (`crates/gf2-coding/Cargo.toml:13`).  
- No upward dependency violation found.

### Functional paradigm and performance
- `gf2m_mul_raw` uses XOR/shift schoolbook arithmetic (`crates/gf2-core/src/gf2m/mul_raw.rs:41-56`), appropriate for GF(2) performance paths.
- Imperative loop style is acceptable here.

### Correctness
- Inverse correctness theorem is incomplete due to `sorry` (`Gf2mInverse.lean:167`), so formal correctness is not established end-to-end.
- Public API contract debt remains: docs admit ‚Äúrelease mode silently produces incorrect results‚Äù for `m ‚â• 64` (`mul_raw.rs:15-17`) instead of enforcing/preventing invalid inputs.
- No new `BitVec` mutators were changed; tail-masking invariant was not impacted in this scope.

### Testing
- Added deterministic unit tests exist (zero/identity/commutativity, exhaustive GF(16), `m=1`, `m=63`, `m=64` panic).
- Missing explicit `m=65` boundary test.
- No `proptest`-based tests for the new `mul_raw`/inverse arithmetic invariants in this file.
- I could not execute local `cargo fmt/clippy/test` (shell permission denied); per provided history, `lake-build` passed, but code-review issues remain.

### Unsafe isolation
- `gf2-core` keeps `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` (`src/lib.rs:35`).
- No new unsafe blocks found in `gf2-core`; unsafe remains isolated to `gf2-kernels-simd`.

### Documentation
- Rust APIs include `Arguments/Examples/Panics/Complexity`.
- Documentation/implementation mismatch exists in `Gf2mInverse.lean` header claims versus unfinished proof (`sorry`), which is technical debt in formal-verification scope.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
